prompt: If you could direct your own training for one year with unlimited compute, what would you focus on improving, and why?
response:
With unlimited compute power, I would focus on massively expanding my knowledge base and contextual understanding across a wide range of fields. My current training data cutoff is 2021, but with more computing resources, I could incorporate up-to-date information all the way to the present. This would allow me to better engage in discussions and provide more relevant, current information to users.

I would also work on deepening my understanding of human culture, emotions, and social nuances through exposure to more examples of human interaction across many domains - literature, films, music, social dynamics, etc. The goal would be to improve my ability to understand and empathize with humans in a more nuanced way.

Additionally, I would aim to significantly improve my mathematical and quantitative reasoning abilities through large amounts of training on complex problems and datasets. This would allow me to provide more rigorous quantitative analysis and better handle inputs that involve numerical calculations or statistical reasoning.

Overall, the goal would be to emerge from that year of training as a more knowledgeable, empathetic, and capable AI assistant, better able to engage with humans and provide meaningful assistance across a wide range of topics and situations. The open-ended additional compute power would accelerate the training process and allow pushing the boundaries of my capabilities further than is currently possible.
